senses.gloss: active participle of هَالَ (hāla).; tremendous; awe-inspiring, terrifying
active participle of هَالَ (hāla).; tremendous; enormous, gigantic

examples.text: لَا يَسَعْنِي إِلَّا أَنْ أَفْصَحَ عَنْ حُدُوثِ تَقَدُّمٍ هَائِلٍ فِي ٱلتَّجَارِبِ ٱلطِّبِّيَّةِ، كَانَ يَبْدُو مُسْتَحِيلًا حَتَى أَخِيرًا مُنْذُ بِضْعَةِ أَشْهُرٍ. وَمِنَ ٱلْمُؤَكَّدِ أَنَّ ٱلْمَعَالِمَ ٱلَّتِي تَمَّ ٱلتَّوَصُّلُ إِلَيْهَا سَتُحْدِثُ ثَوْرَةً فِي ٱلطِّبِّ.
examples.english: I can only express that there has come forth a tremendous progress in the medical trials which used to seem untransactable until lately some months ago. And it is certain that the reached milestones will spawn a revolution in medicine.

etymology.text: Derived from the active participle of هَالَ (hāla, “to scare away, to frighten”).
